first_paragraph: ***Blood Creek*** (previously known as ***Creek*** and ***Town Creek***) is a 2009 American [supernatural horror](/wiki/Supernatural_horror_film "Supernatural horror film") [thriller film](/wiki/Thriller_film "Thriller film") directed by [Joel Schumacher](/wiki/Joel_Schumacher "Joel Schumacher") and written by [David Kajganich](/wiki/David_Kajganich "David Kajganich").[[3]](#cite_note-3) It stars [Dominic Purcell](/wiki/Dominic_Purcell "Dominic Purcell") and [Henry Cavill](/wiki/Henry_Cavill "Henry Cavill") as brothers on a mission of revenge who become trapped in a harrowing [occult](/wiki/Occult "Occult") experiment dating back to the [Third Reich](/wiki/Third_Reich "Third Reich").[[4]](#cite_note-4) The film had a limited theatrical release on September 18, 2009.[[1]](#cite_note-dreadcentral1-1)

***Blood Creek*** (previously known as ***Creek*** and ***Town Creek***) is a 2009 American [supernatural horror](/wiki/Supernatural_horror_film "Supernatural horror film") [thriller film](/wiki/Thriller_film "Thriller film") directed by [Joel Schumacher](/wiki/Joel_Schumacher "Joel Schumacher") and written by [David Kajganich](/wiki/David_Kajganich "David Kajganich").[[3]](#cite_note-3) It stars [Dominic Purcell](/wiki/Dominic_Purcell "Dominic Purcell") and [Henry Cavill](/wiki/Henry_Cavill "Henry Cavill") as brothers on a mission of revenge who become trapped in a harrowing [occult](/wiki/Occult "Occult") experiment dating back to the [Third Reich](/wiki/Third_Reich "Third Reich").[[4]](#cite_note-4) The film had a limited theatrical release on September 18, 2009.[[1]](#cite_note-dreadcentral1-1)

## Plot

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Blood_Creek&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Plot")]

In 1936, a German professor, Richard Wirth, is hosted by the Wollners, a family of German immigrants in [West Virginia](/wiki/West_Virginia "West Virginia"). The Wollners believe him to be a visiting scholar, but Wirth turns out to be a Nazi occultist who seeks a Viking [runestone](/wiki/Runestone "Runestone") buried on their property. When Wirth reveals he wants to use it for evil, he is interrupted by the family, who trap him in their basement and bind him through a ritual that requires frequent human sacrifices. Linked to Wirth, the family survive through the decades, operating as both captors and servants to Wirth, who they keep weakened.

In 2007, 25-year-old [paramedic](/wiki/Paramedic "Paramedic") Evan Marshall is surprised when his older brother Victor suddenly appears after having disappeared during a camping trip in rural West Virginia. Victor explains that he has escaped his captors, and they quickly prepare to return for vengeance. The brothers head to the farm and confront the Wollners. They, in turn, warn the siblings about Wirth. They do not listen until Wirth gets out of the cellar and begins his terror. Wirth reveals that the reason Victor was able to escape was because Wirth knew that Victor would come back to the farm for revenge and would eventually free him from the Wollners, so he let Victor go on purpose. The brothers manage to poison and decapitate Wirth, but as a result the Wollners rapidly turn old and die. Before the youngest dies, she tells Evan that SS leader [Heinrich Himmler](/wiki/Heinrich_Himmler "Heinrich Himmler") has sent eight more Nazi agents to different farms. Evan finds a map that was under the farm and discovers that others like Wirth are at other farms. While Victor returns home to his family, Evan heads out to the other farms to stop the Nazis.

## Cast

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Blood_Creek&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Cast")]

* [Henry Cavill](/wiki/Henry_Cavill "Henry Cavill") as Evan Marshall[[5]](#cite_note-5)
* [Dominic Purcell](/wiki/Dominic_Purcell "Dominic Purcell") as Victor Alan Marshall[[1]](#cite_note-dreadcentral1-1)
* [Emma Booth](/wiki/Emma_Booth_(actress) "Emma Booth (actress)") as Liese Wollner
* [Michael Fassbender](/wiki/Michael_Fassbender "Michael Fassbender") as Richard Wirth
* Rainer Winkelvoss as Otto Wollner
* Laszlo Matray as Karl Wollner
* Joy McBrinn as Mrs. Wollner
* [Shea Whigham](/wiki/Shea_Whigham "Shea Whigham") as Luke
* [Lynn Collins](/wiki/Lynn_Collins "Lynn Collins") as Barb

[Wentworth Miller](/wiki/Wentworth_Miller "Wentworth Miller"), Purcell's *[Prison Break](/wiki/Prison_Break "Prison Break")* co-star, makes an uncredited appearance as a German soldier.

## Production

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Blood_Creek&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Production")]

The script was purchased by [Warner Bros.](/wiki/Warner_Bros. "Warner Bros.") in 2003, after [David Kajganich](/wiki/David_Kajganich "David Kajganich")'s adaptation of *[The Lost Honour of Katharina Blum](/wiki/The_Lost_Honour_of_Katharina_Blum "The Lost Honour of Katharina Blum")* didn't pan out.[[6]](#cite_note-6) [Peter Care](/wiki/Peter_Care "Peter Care") was hired to direct, and was expected to rework the script with Kajganich.[[7]](#cite_note-7) After Warner Bros. put the project in turnaround, [Lionsgate](/wiki/Lionsgate_Films "Lionsgate Films") bought the rights from them, and hired [Joel Schumacher](/wiki/Joel_Schumacher "Joel Schumacher") to direct. Filming was scheduled in March 2007;[[8]](#cite_note-8) production was filmed on locations in [Romania](/wiki/Romania "Romania") ([Bucharest](/wiki/Bucharest "Bucharest") and [MediaPro Studios](/wiki/MediaPro_Studios "MediaPro Studios")).

## Reception

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Blood_Creek&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Reception")]

*Blood Creek* received mixed reviews from critics. On the review aggregator website [Rotten Tomatoes](/wiki/Rotten_Tomatoes "Rotten Tomatoes"), the film holds a 50% rating based on 6 reviews; the average rating is 4.7/10.[[9]](#cite_note-9) [Bloody Disgusting](/wiki/Bloody_Disgusting "Bloody Disgusting") rated the film 3/5 stars and called it "an enjoyable, marginally original, and fast-paced tale, with a franchise-ready villain and some truly entertaining setpieces."[[10]](#cite_note-10) Justin Julian of [Dread Central](/wiki/Dread_Central "Dread Central") rated it 2/5 stars, and, while he praised the villain, stated that the film needed "two or three rewrites ... to tighten the script."[[11]](#cite_note-11)

## Home media

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Blood_Creek&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Home media")]

The DVD was released on January 19, 2010 and features audio commentary from [Joel Schumacher](/wiki/Joel_Schumacher "Joel Schumacher").[[12]](#cite_note-12)
